findings.evidence: `nl -ba` shows the undocumented `show ... from rfl` at line 63; `rg` confirms Mathlib has `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Operator/LinearIsometry.lean:616:theorem coe_toContinuousLinearEquiv : ⇑e.toContinuousLinearEquiv = e`.
findings.fix: Replace the inline `show ... from rfl` with an explicit rewrite using the existing coercion lemma, e.g. `LinearIsometryEquiv.coe_toContinuousLinearEquiv`, or add a short local lemma/comment explaining why this coercion alignment is necessary.
findings.issue: `iteratedFDeriv_comp_linearIsometryEquiv_apply` uses `show (f ∘ ⇑l) = f ∘ ⇑l.toContinuousLinearEquiv from rfl` inside a rewrite, hiding a coercion defeq between `LinearIsometryEquiv` and its continuous-linear-equivalence wrapper.

summary: One proof relies on an undocumented coercion definitional equality in the private iterated-derivative transport lemma; the rest of the proofs are compact and built on named transport lemmas.
